perturbation.original_text: Section 3.01 Right to Use. ETI, on behalf of Entrust, grants to NTL and its ------------ Affiliates (as defined in the Enterprise License) a non-exclusive, fully paid- up, worldwide, perpetual license to use an unlimited number of copies of the Entrust Products subject to the terms and conditions of an agreement to be concluded between NTL and ETI promptly after the Effective Date in substantially the form of the license set forth in Exhibit B (the "Enterprise License") save ------------------ as amended to comply with the provisions of this Article III. NTL represents as of the Effective Date that the terms of the Enterprise License are materially similar to the terms of an existing agreement with a third-party licensee of the Entrust Products, except for the terms relating to price and the provisions of Section 3.02 hereof
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3.01 Right to Use. ETI, on behalf of Entrust, grants to NTL and its ------------ Affiliates (as defined in the Enterprise License) a non-exclusive, fully paid- up, worldwide license to use an unlimited number of copies of the Entrust Products subject to the terms and conditions of an agreement to be concluded between NTL and ETI promptly after the Effective Date in substantially the form of the license set forth in Exhibit B (the "Enterprise License") save ------------------ as amended to comply with the provisions of this Article III. This license is effective for the duration of this agreement only and will be terminated automatically when this agreement terminates. NTL represents as of the Effective Date that the terms of the Enterprise License are materially similar to the terms of an existing agreement with a third-party licensee of the Entrust Products, except for the terms relating to price and the provisions of Section 3.02 hereof
perturbation.explanation: Changing the "perpetual license" to a license that lasts only for the duration of the agreement introduces a contradiction. Section 3.01 initially grants a perpetual license, but the added sentence limits the duration to the agreement's term. This directly contradicts the meaning of "perpetual", creating uncertainty about how long NTL can use the Entrust Products. This change is contradictory to the definition of terms in Article I, where Perpetual License is not defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.01 Right to Use. ETI, on behalf of Entrust, grants to NTL and its ------------ Affiliates (as defined in the Enterprise License) a non-exclusive, fully paid- up, worldwide license to use an unlimited number of copies of the Entrust Products subject to the terms and conditions of an agreement to be concluded between NTL and ETI promptly after the Effective Date in substantially the form of the license set forth in Exhibit B (the "Enterprise License") save ------------------ as amended to comply with the provisions of this Article III. This license is effective for the duration of this agreement only and will be terminated automatically when this agreement terminates. NTL represents as of the Effective Date that the terms of the Enterprise License are materially similar to the terms of an existing agreement with a third-party licensee of the Entrust Products, except for the terms relating to price and the provisions of Section 3.02 hereof
perturbation.justification: The original text grants a "perpetual license." The changed text modifies the same sentence to explicitly state, "This license is effective for the duration of this agreement only and will be terminated automatically when this agreement terminates." This creates a direct contradiction: a license cannot be both perpetual and limited to the agreement's term. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, as the amended section now contains conflicting terms regarding the license's duration. This is a structural flaw causing in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 6.02. Nortel Benefit from Cross Licenses. Subject to the terms and ----------------------------------- conditions of this Agreement, Entrust, to the extent of its legal right to do so, hereby grants to Nortel, under the Entrust Patents, an irrevocable, non- transferable, non-assignable, indivisible, non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide license for Licensed Products and Licensed Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 6.02. Nortel Benefit from Cross Licenses. Subject to the terms and ----------------------------------- conditions of this Agreement, Entrust, to the extent of its legal right to do so, hereby grants to Nortel, under the Entrust Patents, a revocable, non- transferable, non-assignable, indivisible, non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide license for Licensed Products and Licensed Services.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "irrevocable" to "revocable" creates a direct contradiction within Section 6.02 itself. The original text explicitly states the license is irrevocable, meaning it cannot be taken back. Changing it to revocable implies that Entrust *can* terminate the license under certain circumstances. This creates immediate uncertainty about the security of Nortel's license under Entrust's patents, directly contradicting the intention behind the original irrevocable grant. The term "irrevocable" is also a legal term in Article I which is being contradicted due to this perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 6.02. Nortel Benefit from Cross Licenses. Subject to the terms and ----------------------------------- conditions of this Agreement, Entrust, to the extent of its legal right to do so, hereby grants to Nortel, under the Entrust Patents, a revocable, non- transferable, non-assignable, indivisible, non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide license for Licensed Products and Licensed Services.
perturbation.justification: The change from "irrevocable" to "revocable" directly contradicts the original intent of the license grant in Section 6.02. The original text explicitly states the license is irrevocable, meaning it cannot be taken back. Changing it to revocable implies that Entrust *can* terminate the license under certain circumstances. The Changed Text is found in the Contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction that would cause confusion, unenforceability, or a legal issue within the document, as it creates immediate uncertainty about the security of Nortel's license under Entrust's patents. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Section 10.01. Term. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, this ---- Agreement shall terminate on the later of (i) the third anniversary of the Effective Date or (ii) the date on which ETI ceases to be a Subsidiary of NTL.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 10.01. Term. Notwithstanding any other provision in this Agreement, this ---- Agreement shall terminate on the earlier of (i) the tenth anniversary of the Effective Date or (ii) the date on which ETI ceases to be a Subsidiary of NTL. Following the tenth anniversary, this agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least 90 days prior to the end of the then-current term.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the termination conditions introduces a contradiction. The original text states the agreement terminates on the *later* of two dates (3rd anniversary or ETI ceasing to be a subsidiary). The modification changes it to the *earlier* of two dates (10th anniversary or ETI ceasing to be a subsidiary) and adds an automatic renewal clause. This directly contradicts the initial termination date and the absence of any renewal mechanism, creating uncertainty about the actual duration of the agreement. Also this perturbation is in contradiction of Section 6.11 (a).
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Section 6.11. Duration of Cross Licenses. ---------------------------
(a) The Patent License shall commence on the Effective Date hereof (except as provided in Section 6.12) and shall continue for the Patent License Term unless terminated as provided in Article X or Section 6.06. Notwithstanding the expiration of the Patent License Term, the rights and licenses granted hereunder shall continue for the entire terms that the Entrust Patents or the NTL Patents, as the case may be, are in force or for that part of such terms for which the Grantor has the right to grant such rights and licenses. Notwithstanding any other provision in this Agreement, the Patent License shall terminate immediately upon ETI ceasing to be a Subsidiary of NTL.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10.01 introduces an automatic renewal clause and alters the termination condition to the *earlier* of two dates, contradicting Section 6.11(a). Section 6.11(a) specifies that the Patent License shall continue for the Patent License Term unless terminated as provided in Article X or Section 6.06, and terminates immediately upon ETI ceasing to be a Subsidiary of NTL. The added renewal and modified termination condition in Section 10.01 creates an inconsistency with the termination conditions outlined in Section 6.11(a), therefore it's a strong contradiction.
